title: celtis dichotoma
intro: Celtis dichotoma, also known as the Chinese hackberry or Yin Xing, is a deciduous tree native to China and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Celtis dichotoma include lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and improving cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Celtis dichotoma typically involve drying and processing the leaves, bark, or roots, which can be made into teas, capsules, or extracts for medicinal use.
